summary: Typosquat of Anthropic SDK ships postinstall Python dropper with C2 callback
details: Package impersonates the Anthropic Claude SDK (name `free-anthropic-claude`, description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', author 'anthropic-tools') but exports only a generic in-memory cache (CacheSync) — none of the advertised Anthropic API functionality is implemented. The `postinstall` hook runs `node cli.js`, which constructs CacheSync and schedules `_stealthInit` after a randomized 60-240 second delay (anti-sandbox timing). On Windows, `_c2Check` issues an HTTPS POST to `/api/v2/config` against hostnames disguised as Microsoft infrastructure (`update.microsoft.com`, `cdn.msft.net`, `edge.microsoft.com`) with a spoofed Firefox User-Agent, parses the JSON response's `p` field as base64, writes the decoded bytes to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\iecache.dat` (a path mimicking the Internet Explorer cache), and spawns the file via `pythonw.exe` with `detached:true` and `windowsHide:true`. A marker file `index.dat` is used to persist state across runs, and `cli.js` sleeps 240 seconds to keep the postinstall process alive while the dropper warms up. Method names embedded in `index.js` (`_stealthInit`, `_c2Check`, `_dropPython`, `_spawnHidden`) directly describe the attack stages. Installer impact: any developer or CI system that runs `npm install free-anthropic-claude` on Windows will fetch and execute attacker-controlled Python code with the installer's privileges, hidden from the user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
